political.s: <cite index="4-1">In 2023, Visa was named one of the World's Most Ethical Companies by Ethisphere for a 12th consecutive year and maintained a 100% score on the CPA–Zicklin Index of Corporate Political Disclosure and Accountability.</cite> The FEC data shows $907K raised with a bipartisan lean (30% to Republicans, 70% to Democrats/leaners). <cite index="53-1">Visa Inc gave $669,000 to federal candidates in the 2023-2024 election cycle</cite> with <cite index="54-1">Visa Inc spending $8,370,000 lobbying in 2022.</cite>

charity.s: <cite index="12-1">Visa and Visa Foundation make charitable donations and grants to advance the goals and strategic priorities we have set for enabling individuals, businesses, and economies to thrive.</cite> <cite index="13-5">Community giving efforts focus on the needs most relevant to the communities in which we live, work and do business across themes such as access to education, support for vulnerable populations, hunger relief and promotion of good health.</cite> <cite index="14-8,14-10">Grants funded $80 million and Dedicated investments made $120 million in emerging markets helping more women control capital.</cite>
charity.focus: Financial inclusion, women's economic advancement, small business development, disaster relief, food security

environment.s: <cite index="4-18">The company achieved a 24% reduction in scopes 1 & 2 GHG emissions since fiscal year 2020, tracking toward its goal to reach net-zero emissions by 2040, and maintained 100% renewable electricity across offices and data centers.</cite> <cite index="2-2">Visa engages with its top suppliers around their management and reporting of climate change impacts and other environmental issues, including through annual participation in the CDP Supply Chain program.</cite>

labor.s: <cite index="4-16">Visa upheld its commitment to invest in its people and foster a diverse and inclusive workplace, with its efforts including a focus on providing opportunities for growth and development and comprehensive rewards and benefits.</cite> <cite index="2-17">Policies contributing to the respect of human rights of our employees include: Harassment and Discrimination Policy; Safety, Health and Environmental Policy; Whistleblower Policy and Non-Retaliation Policy.</cite> <cite index="35-6">Visa's approximately 34,000 employees earned far less than McInerney, with his compensation being 204 times the $154,909 median pay level of the workforce</cite> (FY2024 data).

dei.s: <cite index="42-2">Visa embraces employees of all identities and continues to offer resources that support an inclusive workplace.</cite> <cite index="41-3">Visa Inc. has a Diversity, Equity & Inclusion rating of 3.9 out of 5 stars, based on 3,249 anonymous employee ratings.</cite> However, <cite index="43-7,43-8">after FEP submitted a shareholder proposal to Visa seeking transparency on DEI programs' ROI, Visa sought to exclude the proposal from proxy materials by arguing it exceeds word limits, resorting to arcane word games.</cite>

animals.s: Not applicable—Visa is a financial services/payment network company with no animal testing or animal welfare policies relevant to core operations.

guns.s: Not applicable—Visa is a financial services/payment network company that does not manufacture or sell firearms.

privacy.s: <cite index="25-3,25-4">Visa operates one of the world's largest payment networks, and respecting privacy is central to its purpose. Visa has a Global Privacy Program to ensure proper safeguards be applied to personal information collected, used and shared.</cite> <cite index="29-17,29-18">Visa published the multi-stakeholder, peer-reviewed Visa Consent Management Guidelines as an industry and organization-agnostic blueprint for building a consented data-sharing ecosystem.</cite> However, <cite index="22-2,22-4,22-5">Visa collects biometric identifiers including facial recognition data and keystroke timing, and infers and derives data elements by analyzing relationships and transactions for marketing, security, or fraud purposes.</cite>

execPay.s: <cite index="34-2,34-14">Visa's CEO McInerney's total pay of $31.56 million was roughly 254 times higher than the median employee's $124,000 annual salary in 2025.</cite> <cite index="38-6,38-7">Compensation was heavily weighted toward variable pay, with 94% ($29.7 million) consisting of incentives and equity awards, allocating approximately 81% of total target direct compensation to long-term incentives.</cite>

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
